question.text: What is the maximum aggregate consolidated borrowing limit for an InvIT as a percentage of the value of InvIT assets?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, the aggregate consolidated borrowings cannot exceed 70% of the value of the InvIT assets. However, borrowing between 49% and 70% requires AAA credit rating, utilization only for acquisition/development of infrastructure projects, unit holder approval, and a track record of 6 distributions. **The Trap:** 49% is the maximum borrowing limit for REITs, not InvITs. InvITs can borrow up to 70% with additional conditions beyond 49%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** InvIT borrowing tiers: up to 25% (normal), 25-49% (credit rating + unit holder approval), 49-70% (AAA rating + restricted use + 6 distributions track record).
